messages.content: Now the conditions are being met. However no green markers are being added to the map at the stations. Should I use ${secondCityCoords[0]},${secondCityCoords[1]} instead of secondCityCoords to create the circle markers
now i wish to add a pop-up for the greenCircleMarker1 - with the content 'Buy the land around ${firstCity} Station to start earning rent every day'
add a button with id 'stationone' and the text 'Buy Land for £100,000' to greenCircleMarker1 popup
in this button event listener I wish to add an if statement if money >= 100000 then color the polylines green for the building footprints 150 meters around firstCity
do i need to fetch the data again, I fetched that data earlier in the code
when the user buys the land I want to add 50,000 to the dailybonus. I think my logic 'dailybonus += 50000;' may be setting the dailybonus to 50000 rather than adding 50,000

If you want to add 50,000 to the existing `dailybonus` value instead of setting it to 50,000, you should use the `+=` compound assignment operator correctly to increment the `dailybonus` by the specified amount. 

Here is the corrected line of code to add 50,000 to the `dailybonus` variable:

```javascript
dailybonus += 50000; // Add 50,000 to the existing dailybonus value
```

This code snippet will increment the `dailybonus` variable by 50,000 without setting it to a fixed value. Make sure to use this corrected line of code within the logic where you want to increase the `dailybonus` by 50,000, such as when the user buys the land.
so my logic is correct

Yes, your logic is correct. The line of code `dailybonus += 50000;` is the correct way to add 50,000 to the existing `dailybonus` value in JavaScript. 

This compound assignment operator (`+=`) is used to add the value specified on the right-hand side of the operator to the variable on the left-hand side and then assign the result back to the variable. In this case, it increments the `dailybonus` variable by 50,000.

You can continue to use this line of code to correctly add 50,000 to the `dailybonus` variable in your application logic. If you encounter any issues or have further questions, feel free to ask!

To change the color of the polylines and add a green fill for the building footprints using Leaflet, you can utilize the `fillColor` property along with the `fillOpacity` property in the options of the `L.polyline` method. 

Here's an updated code snippet to create polylines with green color and fill for the building footprints:

```javascript
// Create a polyline for the building footprint with green color and fill
const polyline = L.polyline(coordinates, {
    color: "green", // Set the color of the polyline to green
    weight: 1,
    fillColor: "green", // Set the fill color to green
    fillOpacity: 0.5, // Set the opacity of the fill color
}).addTo(map);
```

In this code snippet:
- The `color` property is set to "green" to change the color of the polyline.
- The `fillColor` property is also set to "green" to specify the fill color of the polygonal area enclosed by the polyline.
- The `fillOpacity` property is set to 0.5 to adjust the opacity level of the fill color (where 1 is fully opaque and 0 is fully transparent).

Using these options in the `L.polyline` method, you can create polylines with a green color and fill for the building footprints on the map. Adjust the fillOpacity value as needed to achieve the desired visual effect for the building footprints.
